content.problemStatement:
Initially, Chef had an array AAA of length NNN. Chef performs the following operation on AAA **at most** once:


Select LLL and RRR such that 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N and set Ai:=Ai+1A_i := A_i + 1Ai‚Äã:=Ai‚Äã+1 for all L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R.
Determine the **maximum** number of inversions Chef can decrease from the array AAA by applying the operation **at most** once.

More formally, let the final array obtained after applying the operation **at most** once be BBB. You need to determine the **maximum** value of inv(A)‚àíinv(B)inv(A) - inv(B)inv(A)‚àíinv(B) (where inv(X)inv(X)inv(X) denotes the number of inversions in array XXX).

**Note:** The number of inversions in an array XXX is the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Xi>XjX_i \gt X_jXi‚Äã>Xj‚Äã.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** value of inv(A)‚àíinv(B)inv(A) - inv(B)inv(A)‚àíinv(B) which can be obtained after applying at most one operation.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The initial array AAA is [4,2,3,1,5][4, 2, 3, 1, 5][4,2,3,1,5] which has 555 inversions. We can perform operation on L=3,R=4L = 3, R = 4L=3,R=4. The resultant array will be [4,2,4,2,5][4, 2, 4, 2, 5][4,2,4,2,5] which has 333 inversions. Therefore we reduce the number of inversion by 222 which is the maximum decrement possible.
Test case 222: The initial array AAA is [1,2,3,4,5,6][1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6][1,2,3,4,5,6] which has 000 inversions. In this case, we do not need to apply any operation and the final array BBB will be same as the initial array AAA. Therefore the maximum possible decrement in inversions is 000.
Test case 333: The initial array AAA is [2,1,1,1][2, 1, 1, 1][2,1,1,1] which has 333 inversions. We can perform operation on L=2,R=4L = 2, R = 4L=2,R=4. The resultant array will be [2,2,2,2][2, 2, 2, 2][2,2,2,2] which has 000 inversions. Therefore we reduce the number of inversion by 333 which is the maximum decrement possible.
